LAW AND MOTION, DEFENDANT CHRISTINA M. WENDEL, PH.D.'S MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT AND TO DISMISS THE ACTION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO STRIKE AS TO THE PRAYER FOR (ATTORNEYS) FEES AND PUNITIVE DAMAGES IS GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO OBTAIN THE REQUIRED ORDER TO SEEK THOSE DAMAGES. CENTRAL PATHOLOGY SERVICE MEDICAL CLINIC, INC. V. SUPERIOR COURT (1992) 3 CAL.4TH 181. THE MOTION IS DENIED ON THE REMAINING GROUNDS. MOVING PARTY TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER (TO INCLUDE SAME LANGUAGE AS OTHER ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT, 15 DAYS TO ANSWER FROM NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER). (302)

MINI MINUTES FOR MAR-21-2012 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT WILLIAM QUICK'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED AND ALTERNATE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED ON THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF TRUST AND THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUD. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER DR. QUICK BREACHED THE STANDARD OF CARE AND WHETHER THAT BREACH CAUSED PLAINTIFF DAMAGES. AS TO THE BREACH OF TRUST CLAIM, DR. QUICK SATISFIED HIS BURDEN OF PRODUCTION AND PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PRESENTED ANY EVIDENCE SHOWING THAT DEFENDANT WAS PLACED HIS FINANCIAL INTERESTS OVER THE INTERESTS OF PLAINTIFF. AS TO THE FRAUD CLAIM, DR. QUICK SATISFIED HIS BURDEN OF PRODUCTION AND PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PRESENTED ANY EVIDENCE SHOWING THAT DEFENDANT HAD A FRAUDULENT INTENT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CHRISTINA WENDEL'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED AND ALTERNATE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION DENIED ON THE NEGLIGENCE CLAIM AND GRANTED ON THE BREACH OF TRUST AND FRAUD CLAIMS. THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES WHETHER DEFENDANT BREACHED THE STANDARD OF CARE AND THAT BREACH CAUSED PLAINTIFF DAMAGES. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT PRESENT ANY EVIDENCE THAT DEFENDANT PLACING HIS OWN FINANCIAL INTERESTS OVER THE INTERESTS OF PLAINTIFF. NOR DOES PLAINTIFF IDENTIFY AN ACTIONABLE MISREPRESENTATION. THE FOUNDATION OBJECTIONS TO THE EXPERT DECLARATIONS ARE OVERRULED. DEFENDANT TO EMAIL A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER TO THE COURT AT CONTESTDEPT302@SFTC.ORG. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-27-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CHRISTINA WENDEL'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.
Appearances: Kathleen A. Humphrey of Supple & Canvel, LLP (366.5533) for defendant and moving party; Evan Selik of Brentwood Legal Services (310.442.6042 x1) appearing via Court Call for plaintiff and responding party.

Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: the motion for summary judgment is denied and alternate motion for summary adjudication denied on the negligence claim and granted on the breach of trust and fraud claims. There are triable issues whether defendant breached the standard of care and that breach caused plaintiff damages. Plaintiff does not present any evidence that defendant placing his own financial interests over the interests of plaintiff. Nor does Plaintiff identify an actionable misrepresentation. The foundation objections to the expert declarations are overruled. Defendant to email a proposed form of order to the court at contestdept302@sftc.org. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Dec-20-2011 Continued To May-29-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-21-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Joseph C. Gharrity, Esq., of Hassard Bonnington LLP, Tel. 415.288.9800, appearing for Defendant and moving party.

No other appearance. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT WILLIAM QUICK'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - Motion for summary judgment is denied and alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted on the third cause of action for breach of trust and the fourth cause of action for fraud. There is a triable issue whether Dr. Quick breached the standard of care and whether that breach caused plaintiff damages. As to the breach of trust claim, Dr. Quick satisfied his burden of production and plaintiff has not presented any evidence showing that defendant was placed his financial interests over the interests of plaintiff. As to the fraud claim, Dr. Quick satisfied his burden of production and plaintiff has not presented any evidence showing that defendant had a fraudulent intent. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported.
